1	/********************************************************************
2	 * This file includes functions that are used to organize memories
3	 * in the top module of FPGA fabric
4	 *******************************************************************/
5	#include <cmath>
6	#include <random>
7	
8	/* Headers from vtrutil library */
9	#include "vtr_assert.h"
10	#include "vtr_log.h"
11	#include "vtr_time.h"
12	
13	/* Headers from vpr library */
14	#include "physical_types_util.h"
15	#include "vpr_utils.h"
16	
17	/* Headers from openfpgashell library */
18	#include "build_decoder_modules.h"
19	#include "build_top_module_memory.h"
20	#include "build_top_module_memory_bank.h"
21	#include "command_exit_codes.h"
22	#include "decoder_library_utils.h"
23	#include "memory_bank_utils.h"
24	#include "memory_utils.h"
25	#include "module_manager_utils.h"
26	#include "openfpga_naming.h"
27	#include "openfpga_reserved_words.h"
28	#include "rr_gsb_utils.h"
29	
30	/* begin namespace openfpga */
31	namespace openfpga {
32	
33	/********************************************************************
34	 * This function adds the CBX/CBY of a tile
35	 * to the memory modules and memory instances
36	 * This function is designed for organizing memory modules in top-level
37	 * module
38	 *******************************************************************/
39	static void organize_top_module_tile_cb_modules(
40	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
41	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib,
42	  const e_config_protocol_type& sram_orgz_type,
43	  const CircuitModelId& sram_model, const vtr::Matrix<size_t>& cb_instance_ids,
44	  const DeviceRRGSB& device_rr_gsb, const RRGSB& rr_gsb,
45	  const e_rr_type& cb_type, const bool& compact_routing_hierarchy) {
46	  /* If the CB does not exist, we can skip addition */
47	  if (false == rr_gsb.is_cb_exist(cb_type)) {
48	    return;
49	  }
50	
51	  /* Skip if the cb does not contain any configuration bits! */
52	  if (true == connection_block_contain_only_routing_tracks(rr_gsb, cb_type)) {
53	    return;
54	  }
55	
56	  vtr::Point<size_t> cb_coord(rr_gsb.get_cb_x(cb_type),
57	                              rr_gsb.get_cb_y(cb_type));
58	  /* If we use compact routing hierarchy, we should instanciate the unique
59	   * module of SB */
60	  if (true == compact_routing_hierarchy) {
61	    /* Note: use GSB coordinate when inquire for unique modules!!! */
62	    const RRGSB& unique_mirror = device_rr_gsb.get_cb_unique_module(
63	      cb_type, vtr::Point<size_t>(rr_gsb.get_x(), rr_gsb.get_y()));
64	    cb_coord.set_x(unique_mirror.get_cb_x(cb_type));
65	    cb_coord.set_y(unique_mirror.get_cb_y(cb_type));
66	  }
67	
68	  std::string cb_module_name =
69	    generate_connection_block_module_name(cb_type, cb_coord);
70	  ModuleId cb_module = module_manager.find_module(cb_module_name);
71	  VTR_ASSERT(true == module_manager.valid_module_id(cb_module));
72	
73	  /* Identify if this sub module includes configuration bits,
74	   * we will update the memory module and instance list
75	   */
76	  if (0 < find_module_num_config_bits(module_manager, cb_module, circuit_lib,
77	                                      sram_model, sram_orgz_type)) {
78	    /* CBX coordinate conversion calculation: (1,0) -> (2,1) */
79	    vtr::Point<int> config_coord(rr_gsb.get_cb_x(cb_type) * 2,
80	                                 rr_gsb.get_cb_y(cb_type) * 2 + 1);
81	    if (cb_type == e_rr_type::CHANY) {
82	      /* CBY has a different coordinate conversion calculation: (0,1) -> (1,2)
83	       */
84	      config_coord.set(rr_gsb.get_cb_x(cb_type) * 2 + 1,
85	                       rr_gsb.get_cb_y(cb_type) * 2);
86	    }
87	    /* Note that use the original CB coodinate for instance id searching ! */
88	    module_manager.add_configurable_child(
89	      top_module, cb_module,
90	      cb_instance_ids[rr_gsb.get_cb_x(cb_type)][rr_gsb.get_cb_y(cb_type)],
91	      ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::UNIFIED, config_coord);
92	  }
93	}
94	
95	/********************************************************************
96	 * This function adds the SB, CBX, CBY and Grid of a tile
97	 * to the memory modules and memory instances
98	 * This function is designed for organizing memory modules in top-level
99	 * module
100	 * This function also adds coordindates for each configurable child under the
101	 top-level module
102	 * of a FPGA fabric. A configurable child could be a programmable block (grid),
103	 * a Connection Block (CBx/y) or a Switch block (SB).
104	 * This function, we consider a coordinate system as follows
105	 * - Each row may consist of either (1) grid and CBy or (2) CBx and SB
106	 * - Each column may consist of either (1) grid and CBx or (2) CBy and SB
107	 *
108	 *          Column 0      Column 1
109	 *
110	 *     +---------------+----------+
111	 *     |               |          |
112	 *     |               |          |
113	 *     |     Grid      |   CBY    |   Row 3
114	 *     |               |          |
115	 *     |               |          |
116	 *     +---------------+----------+
117	 *     |               |          |
118	 *     |     CBX       |   SB     |   Row 2
119	 *     |               |          |
120	 *     +---------------+----------+
121	 *     |               |          |
122	 *     |               |          |
123	 *     |     Grid      |   CBY    |   Row 1
124	 *     |               |          |
125	 *     |               |          |
126	 *     +---------------+----------+
127	 *     |               |          |
128	 *     |     CBX       |   SB     |   Row 0
129	 *     |               |          |
130	 *     +---------------+----------+
131	
132	 *******************************************************************/
133	static void organize_top_module_tile_memory_modules(
134	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
135	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib,
136	  const e_config_protocol_type& sram_orgz_type,
137	  const CircuitModelId& sram_model, const DeviceGrid& grids,
138	  const vtr::Matrix<size_t>& grid_instance_ids,
139	  const DeviceRRGSB& device_rr_gsb, const vtr::Matrix<size_t>& sb_instance_ids,
140	  const std::map<e_rr_type, vtr::Matrix<size_t>>& cb_instance_ids,
141	  const bool& compact_routing_hierarchy, const size_t& layer,
142	  const vtr::Point<size_t>& tile_coord, const e_side& tile_border_side) {
143	  vtr::Point<size_t> gsb_coord_range = device_rr_gsb.get_gsb_range();
144	
145	  vtr::Point<size_t> gsb_coord(tile_coord.x(), tile_coord.y());
146	
147	  /* We do NOT consider SB and CBs if the gsb is not in the range! */
148	  if ((gsb_coord.x() < gsb_coord_range.x()) &&
149	      (gsb_coord.y() < gsb_coord_range.y())) {
150	    const RRGSB& rr_gsb = device_rr_gsb.get_gsb(gsb_coord.x(), gsb_coord.y());
151	    /* Find Switch Block: unique module id and instance id!
152	     * Note that switch block does always exist in a GSB
153	     */
154	    vtr::Point<size_t> sb_coord(rr_gsb.get_sb_x(), rr_gsb.get_sb_y());
155	    /* If we use compact routing hierarchy, we should instanciate the unique
156	     * module of SB */
157	    if (true == compact_routing_hierarchy) {
158	      const RRGSB& unique_mirror = device_rr_gsb.get_sb_unique_module(sb_coord);
159	      sb_coord.set_x(unique_mirror.get_sb_x());
160	      sb_coord.set_y(unique_mirror.get_sb_y());
161	    }
162	    std::string sb_module_name = generate_switch_block_module_name(sb_coord);
163	    ModuleId sb_module = module_manager.find_module(sb_module_name);
164	    VTR_ASSERT(true == module_manager.valid_module_id(sb_module));
165	
166	    /* Identify if this sub module includes configuration bits,
167	     * we will update the memory module and instance list
168	     */
169	    /* If the CB does not exist, we can skip addition */
170	    if (true == device_rr_gsb.is_sb_exist(gsb_coord)) {
171	      if (0 < find_module_num_config_bits(module_manager, sb_module,
172	                                          circuit_lib, sram_model,
173	                                          sram_orgz_type)) {
174	        vtr::Point<int> config_coord(rr_gsb.get_sb_x() * 2 + 1,
175	                                     rr_gsb.get_sb_y() * 2 + 1);
176	        module_manager.add_configurable_child(
177	          top_module, sb_module,
178	          sb_instance_ids[rr_gsb.get_sb_x()][rr_gsb.get_sb_y()],
179	          ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::UNIFIED, config_coord);
180	      }
181	    }
182	
183	    if (false == module_manager.group_routing()) {
184	      /* Try to find and add CBX and CBY */
185	      organize_top_module_tile_cb_modules(
186	        module_manager, top_module, circuit_lib, sram_orgz_type, sram_model,
187	        cb_instance_ids.at(e_rr_type::CHANX), device_rr_gsb, rr_gsb,
188	        e_rr_type::CHANX, compact_routing_hierarchy);
189	
190	      organize_top_module_tile_cb_modules(
191	        module_manager, top_module, circuit_lib, sram_orgz_type, sram_model,
192	        cb_instance_ids.at(e_rr_type::CHANY), device_rr_gsb, rr_gsb,
193	        e_rr_type::CHANY, compact_routing_hierarchy);
194	    }
195	  }
196	
197	  /* Find the module name for this type of grid */
198	  t_physical_tile_loc phy_tile_loc(tile_coord.x(), tile_coord.y(), layer);
199	  t_physical_tile_type_ptr grid_type = grids.get_physical_type(phy_tile_loc);
200	
201	  /* Skip EMPTY Grid */
202	  if (true == is_empty_type(grid_type)) {
203	    return;
204	  }
205	  /* Skip width > 1 or height > 1 Grid, which should already been processed when
206	   * offset=0 */
207	  if ((0 < grids.get_width_offset(phy_tile_loc)) ||
208	      (0 < grids.get_height_offset(phy_tile_loc))) {
209	    return;
210	  }
211	
212	  std::string grid_module_name_prefix(GRID_MODULE_NAME_PREFIX);
213	  std::string grid_module_name = generate_grid_block_module_name(
214	    grid_module_name_prefix, std::string(grid_type->name), grid_type->is_io(),
215	    tile_border_side);
216	  ModuleId grid_module = module_manager.find_module(grid_module_name);
217	  VTR_ASSERT(true == module_manager.valid_module_id(grid_module));
218	
219	  /* Identify if this sub module includes configuration bits,
220	   * we will update the memory module and instance list
221	   */
222	  if (0 < find_module_num_config_bits(module_manager, grid_module, circuit_lib,
223	                                      sram_model, sram_orgz_type)) {
224	    vtr::Point<int> config_coord(tile_coord.x() * 2, tile_coord.y() * 2);
225	    module_manager.add_configurable_child(
226	      top_module, grid_module,
227	      grid_instance_ids[tile_coord.x()][tile_coord.y()],
228	      ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::UNIFIED, config_coord);
229	  }
230	}
231	
232	/********************************************************************
233	 * Split memory modules into different configurable regions
234	 * This function will create regions based on the definition
235	 * in the configuration protocols, to accommodate each configurable
236	 * child under the top-level module
237	 *
238	 * For example:
239	 *  FPGA Top-level module
240	 *  +----------------------+
241	 *  |           |          |
242	 *  |  Region 0 | Region 1 |
243	 *  |           |          |
244	 *  +----------------------+
245	 *  |           |          |
246	 *  |  Region 2 | Region 3 |
247	 *  |           |          |
248	 *  +----------------------+
249	 *
250	 *  A typical organization of a Region X
251	 *  +-----------------------+
252	 *  |                       |
253	 *  | +------+ +------+     |
254	 *  | |      | |      |     |
255	 *  | | Tile | | Tile | ... |
256	 *  | |      | |      |     |
257	 *  | +------+ +------+     |
258	 *  |  ...      ...         |
259	 *  |                       |
260	 *  | +------+ +------+     |
261	 *  | |      | |      |     |
262	 *  | | Tile | | Tile | ... |
263	 *  | |      | |      |     |
264	 *  | +------+ +------+     |
265	 *  +-----------------------+
266	 *
267	 * Note:
268	 *   - This function should NOT modify configurable children
269	 *
270	 *******************************************************************/
271	void build_top_module_configurable_regions(
272	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
273	  const ConfigProtocol& config_protocol) {
274	  vtr::ScopedStartFinishTimer timer(
275	    "Build configurable regions for the top module");
276	
277	  /* Ensure we have valid configurable children */
278	  VTR_ASSERT(false ==
279	             module_manager
280	               .configurable_children(
281	                 top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
282	               .empty());
283	
284	  /* Ensure that our region definition is valid */
285	  VTR_ASSERT(1 <= config_protocol.num_regions());
286	
287	  /* Exclude decoders from the list */
288	  size_t num_configurable_children =
289	    module_manager
290	      .configurable_children(top_module,
291	                             ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
292	      .size();
293	  if (CONFIG_MEM_MEMORY_BANK == config_protocol.type() ||
294	      CONFIG_MEM_QL_MEMORY_BANK == config_protocol.type()) {
295	    num_configurable_children -= 2;
296	  } else if (CONFIG_MEM_FRAME_BASED == config_protocol.type()) {
297	    num_configurable_children -= 1;
298	  }
299	
300	  /* Evenly place each configurable child to each region */
301	  size_t num_children_per_region =
302	    num_configurable_children / config_protocol.num_regions();
303	  size_t region_child_counter = 0;
304	  bool create_region = true;
305	  ConfigRegionId curr_region = ConfigRegionId::INVALID();
306	  for (size_t ichild = 0;
307	       ichild < module_manager
308	                  .configurable_children(
309	                    top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
310	                  .size();
311	       ++ichild) {
312	    if (true == create_region) {
313	      curr_region = module_manager.add_config_region(top_module);
314	    }
315	
316	    /* Add the child to a region */
317	    module_manager.add_configurable_child_to_region(
318	      top_module, curr_region,
319	      module_manager.configurable_children(
320	        top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)[ichild],
321	      module_manager.configurable_child_instances(
322	        top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)[ichild],
323	      ichild);
324	
325	    /* See if the current region is full or not:
326	     * For the last region, we will keep adding until we finish all the children
327	     */
328	    region_child_counter++;
329	    if (region_child_counter < num_children_per_region) {
330	      create_region = false;
331	    } else if (size_t(curr_region) <
332	               (size_t)config_protocol.num_regions() - 1) {
333	      create_region = true;
334	      region_child_counter = 0;
335	    }
336	  }
337	
338	  /* Ensure that the number of configurable regions created matches the
339	   * definition */
340	  VTR_ASSERT((size_t)config_protocol.num_regions() ==
341	             module_manager.regions(top_module).size());
342	}
343	
344	/********************************************************************
345	 * Organize the list of memory modules and instances
346	 * This function will record all the sub modules of the top-level module
347	 * (those have memory ports) to two lists:
348	 * 1. memory_modules records the module ids
349	 * 2. memory_instances records the instance ids
350	 * To keep a clean memory connection between sub modules and top-level module,
351	 * the sequence of memory_modules and memory_instances will follow
352	 * a chain of tiles considering their physical location
353	 *
354	 * Inter-tile connection:
355	 *
356	 * Inter-tile connection always start from the I/O peripherals
357	 * and the core tiles (CLBs and heterogeneous blocks).
358	 * The sequence of configuration memory will be organized as follows:
359	 *   - I/O peripherals
360	 *     - BOTTOM side (From left to right)
361	 *     - RIGHT side (From bottom to top)
362	 *     - TOP side (From left to right)
363	 *     - LEFT side (From top to bottom)
364	 *   - Core tiles
365	 *     - Tiles at the bottom row, i.e., Tile[0..i] (From left to right)
366	 *     - One row upper, i.e. Tile[i+1 .. j] (From right to left)
367	 *     - Repeat until we finish all the rows
368	 *
369	 *   Note: the tail may not always be on the top-right corner as shown in the
370	 *figure. It may exit at the top-left corner. This really depends on the number
371	 *of rows your have in the core tile array.
372	 *
373	 *   Note: the organization of inter-tile aims to reduce the wire length
374	 *         to connect between tiles. Therefore, it is organized as a snake
375	 *         where we can avoid long wires between rows and columns
376	 *   Note: Corner I/Os only occur when perimeter cb is allowed
377	 *
378	 *    +--------------------------------------------------------+
379	 *    |  +------+    +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
380	 *    |  | I/O  |    | I/O  | I/O  | ... | I/O  |   | I/O  |   |
381	 *    |  | LEFT |    | TOP  | TOP  |     | TOP  |   | TOP  |   |
382	 *    |  +------+    +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
383	 *    |           +---------------------------------->tail     |
384	 *    |  +------+ |  +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
385	 *    |  |      | |  |      |      |     |      |   |      |   |
386	 *    |  | I/O  | |  | Tile | Tile | ... | Tile |   | I/O  |   |
387	 *    |  | LEFT | |  | [h+1]| [h+2]|     |  [n] |   |RIGHT |   |
388	 *    |  +------+ |  +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
389	 *    |           +-------------------------------+            |
390	 *    |    ...         ...    ...    ...   ...    |   ...      |
391	 *    |           +-------------------------------+            |
392	 *    |  +------+ |  +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
393	 *    |  |      | |  |      |      |     |      |   |      |   |
394	 *    |  | I/O  | |  | Tile | Tile | ... | Tile |   | I/O  |   |
395	 *    |  | LEFT | |  | [i+1]| [i+2]|     | [j]  |   |RIGHT |   |
396	 *    |  +------+ |  +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
397	 *    |           +-------------------------------+            |
398	 *    |  +------+    +------+------+-----+------+ | +------+   |
399	 *    |  |      |    |      |      |     |      | | |      |   |
400	 *    |  | I/O  |    | Tile | Tile | ... | Tile | | | I/O  |   |
401	 *    |  | LEFT |    | [0]  |  [1] |     |  [i] | | |RIGHT |   |
402	 *    |  +------+    +------+------+-----+------+ | +------+   |
403	 *    +-------------------------------------------+            |
404	 *       +------+    +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
405	 *       | I/O  |    | I/O  | I/O  | ... | I/O  |   | I/O  |   |
406	 *       |BOTTOM|    |BOTTOM|BOTTOM|     |BOTTOM|   |RIGHT |   |
407	 *       +------+    +------+------+-----+------+   +------+   |
408	 *        head >-----------------------------------------------+
409	 *
410	 * Inner tile connection:
411	 *
412	 *   Inside each tile, the configuration memory will be organized
413	 *   in the following sequence:
414	 *     - Switch Block (SB)
415	 *     - X-directional Connection Block (CBX)
416	 *     - Y-directional Connection Block (CBY)
417	 *     - Configurable Logic Block (CLB), which could also be heterogeneous
418	 *blocks
419	 *
420	 *   Note:
421	 *     Due to multi-column and multi-width hetergeoenous blocks,
422	 *     each tile may not have one or more of SB, CBX, CBY, CLB
423	 *     In such case, the sequence will be respected.
424	 *     The missing block will just be skipped when organizing the configuration
425	 *memories.
426	 *
427	 *       Tile
428	 *     +---------------+----------+
429	 *   <-+---------------+ +        |
430	 *     |               | |        |
431	 *     |     CLB       | | CBY    |
432	 *     |             +-|-+        |
433	 *     |             | |          |
434	 *     +---------------+----------+
435	 *     |             +-+----+-----+---<---
436	 *     |     CBX       |   SB     |
437	 *     |               |          |
438	 *     +---------------+----------+
439	 *
440	 *******************************************************************/
441	void organize_top_module_memory_modules(
442	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
443	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, const ConfigProtocol& config_protocol,
444	  const CircuitModelId& sram_model, const DeviceGrid& grids,
445	  const size_t& layer, const vtr::Matrix<size_t>& grid_instance_ids,
446	  const DeviceRRGSB& device_rr_gsb, const vtr::Matrix<size_t>& sb_instance_ids,
447	  const std::map<e_rr_type, vtr::Matrix<size_t>>& cb_instance_ids,
448	  const bool& compact_routing_hierarchy) {
449	  /* Ensure clean vectors to return */
450	  VTR_ASSERT(true ==
451	             module_manager
452	               .configurable_children(
453	                 top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
454	               .empty());
455	
456	  /* First, organize the I/O tiles on the border */
457	  /* Special for the I/O tileas on RIGHT and BOTTOM,
458	   * which are only I/O blocks, which do NOT contain CBs and SBs
459	   */
460	  std::vector<e_side> io_sides{BOTTOM, RIGHT, TOP, LEFT};
461	  std::map<e_side, std::vector<vtr::Point<size_t>>> io_coords;
462	
463	  /* BOTTOM side I/Os */
464	  for (size_t ix = 0; ix < grids.width() - 1; ++ix) {
465	    io_coords[BOTTOM].push_back(vtr::Point<size_t>(ix, 0));
466	  }
467	
468	  /* RIGHT side I/Os */
469	  for (size_t iy = 0; iy < grids.height() - 1; ++iy) {
470	    io_coords[RIGHT].push_back(vtr::Point<size_t>(grids.width() - 1, iy));
471	  }
472	
473	  /* TOP side I/Os
474	   * Special case for TOP side: We need tile at ix = 0, which has a SB!!!
475	   *
476	   *  TOP-LEFT CORNER of FPGA fabric
477	   *
478	   *    +--------+ +-------+
479	   *    | EMPTY  | | EMPTY |
480	   *    | Grid   | |  CBX  |
481	   *    | [0][x] | |       |
482	   *    +--------+ +-------+
483	   *    +--------+ +--------+
484	   *    | EMPTY  | |  SB    |
485	   *    | CBX    | | [0][x] |
486	   *    +--------+ +--------+
487	   *
488	   */
489	  for (size_t ix = grids.width() - 1; ix >= 1; --ix) {
490	    io_coords[TOP].push_back(vtr::Point<size_t>(ix, grids.height() - 1));
491	  }
492	
493	  /* LEFT side I/Os */
494	  for (size_t iy = grids.height() - 1; iy >= 1; --iy) {
495	    io_coords[LEFT].push_back(vtr::Point<size_t>(0, iy));
496	  }
497	
498	  for (const e_side& io_side : io_sides) {
499	    for (const vtr::Point<size_t>& io_coord : io_coords[io_side]) {
500	      /* Identify the GSB that surrounds the grid */
501	      organize_top_module_tile_memory_modules(
502	        module_manager, top_module, circuit_lib, config_protocol.type(),
503	        sram_model, grids, grid_instance_ids, device_rr_gsb, sb_instance_ids,
504	        cb_instance_ids, compact_routing_hierarchy, layer, io_coord, io_side);
505	    }
506	  }
507	
508	  /* For the core grids */
509	  std::vector<vtr::Point<size_t>> core_coords;
510	  bool positive_direction = true;
511	  for (size_t iy = 1; iy < grids.height() - 1; ++iy) {
512	    /* For positive direction: -----> */
513	    if (true == positive_direction) {
514	      for (size_t ix = 1; ix < grids.width() - 1; ++ix) {
515	        core_coords.push_back(vtr::Point<size_t>(ix, iy));
516	      }
517	    } else {
518	      VTR_ASSERT(false == positive_direction);
519	      /* For negative direction: -----> */
520	      for (size_t ix = grids.width() - 2; ix >= 1; --ix) {
521	        core_coords.push_back(vtr::Point<size_t>(ix, iy));
522	      }
523	    }
524	    /* Flip the positive direction to be negative */
525	    positive_direction = !positive_direction;
526	  }
527	
528	  for (const vtr::Point<size_t>& core_coord : core_coords) {
529	    organize_top_module_tile_memory_modules(
530	      module_manager, top_module, circuit_lib, config_protocol.type(),
531	      sram_model, grids, grid_instance_ids, device_rr_gsb, sb_instance_ids,
532	      cb_instance_ids, compact_routing_hierarchy, layer, core_coord,
533	      NUM_2D_SIDES);
534	  }
535	
536	  /* Split memory modules into different regions */
537	  build_top_module_configurable_regions(module_manager, top_module,
538	                                        config_protocol);
539	}
540	
541	/********************************************************************
542	 * Shuffle the configurable children in a random sequence
543	 *
544	 * TODO: May use a more customized shuffle mechanism
545	 * TODO: Apply region-based shuffling
546	 *       The shuffling will be applied to each separated regions
547	 *       Configurable children will not shuffled from a region
548	 *       to another, instead they should stay in the same region
549	 *
550	 * Note:
551	 *   - This function should NOT be called
552	 *     before allocating any configurable child
553	 ********************************************************************/
554	void shuffle_top_module_configurable_children(
555	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
556	  const ConfigProtocol& config_protocol) {
557	  size_t num_keys =
558	    module_manager
559	      .configurable_children(top_module,
560	                             ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
561	      .size();
562	  std::vector<size_t> shuffled_keys;
563	  shuffled_keys.reserve(num_keys);
564	  for (size_t ikey = 0; ikey < num_keys; ++ikey) {
565	    shuffled_keys.push_back(ikey);
566	  }
567	
568	#ifdef _WIN32
569	  static std::mt19937 rng(std::random_device{}());
570	  std::shuffle(shuffled_keys.begin(), shuffled_keys.end(), rng);
571	#else
572	  std::random_shuffle(shuffled_keys.begin(), shuffled_keys.end());
573	#endif
574	
575	  /* Cache the configurable children and their instances */
576	  std::vector<ModuleId> orig_configurable_children =
577	    module_manager.configurable_children(
578	      top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL);
579	  std::vector<size_t> orig_configurable_child_instances =
580	    module_manager.configurable_child_instances(
581	      top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL);
582	  std::vector<vtr::Point<int>> orig_configurable_child_coordinates =
583	    module_manager.configurable_child_coordinates(
584	      top_module, ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL);
585	
586	  /* Reorganize the configurable children */
587	  module_manager.clear_configurable_children(top_module);
588	
589	  for (size_t ikey = 0; ikey < num_keys; ++ikey) {
590	    module_manager.add_configurable_child(
591	      top_module, orig_configurable_children[shuffled_keys[ikey]],
592	      orig_configurable_child_instances[shuffled_keys[ikey]],
593	      ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::UNIFIED,
594	      orig_configurable_child_coordinates[shuffled_keys[ikey]]);
595	  }
596	
597	  /* Reset configurable regions */
598	  module_manager.clear_config_region(top_module);
599	  build_top_module_configurable_regions(module_manager, top_module,
600	                                        config_protocol);
601	}
602	
603	/********************************************************************
604	 * Load configurable children from a fabric key to top-level module
605	 *
606	 * Note:
607	 *   - This function will overwrite any exisiting configurable children
608	 *     under the top module
609	 *
610	 * Return 0 - Success
611	 * Return 1 - Fatal errors
612	 ********************************************************************/
613	int load_top_module_memory_modules_from_fabric_key(
614	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
615	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, const ConfigProtocol& config_protocol,
616	  const FabricKey& fabric_key) {
617	  /* Ensure a clean start */
618	  module_manager.clear_configurable_children(top_module);
619	
620	  size_t curr_configurable_child_id = 0;
621	
622	  for (const FabricRegionId& region : fabric_key.regions()) {
623	    /* Create a configurable region in the top module */
624	    ConfigRegionId top_module_config_region =
625	      module_manager.add_config_region(top_module);
626	    for (const FabricKeyId& key : fabric_key.region_keys(region)) {
627	      /* Find if instance id is valid */
628	      std::pair<ModuleId, size_t> instance_info(ModuleId::INVALID(), 0);
629	      /* If we have an alias, we try to find a instance in this name */
630	      if (!fabric_key.key_alias(key).empty()) {
631	        /* If we have the key, we can quickly spot instance id.
632	         * Otherwise, we have to exhaustively find the module id and instance id
633	         */
634	        if (!fabric_key.key_name(key).empty()) {
635	          instance_info.first =
636	            module_manager.find_module(fabric_key.key_name(key));
637	          instance_info.second = module_manager.instance_id(
638	            top_module, instance_info.first, fabric_key.key_alias(key));
639	        } else {
640	          instance_info = find_module_manager_instance_module_info(
641	            module_manager, top_module, fabric_key.key_alias(key));
642	        }
643	      } else {
644	        /* If we do not have an alias, we use the name and value to build the
645	         * info deck */
646	        instance_info.first =
647	          module_manager.find_module(fabric_key.key_name(key));
648	        instance_info.second = fabric_key.key_value(key);
649	      }
650	
651	      if (false == module_manager.valid_module_id(instance_info.first)) {
652	        if (!fabric_key.key_alias(key).empty()) {
653	          VTR_LOG_ERROR("Invalid key alias '%s'!\n",
654	                        fabric_key.key_alias(key).c_str());
655	        } else {
656	          VTR_LOG_ERROR("Invalid key name '%s'!\n",
657	                        fabric_key.key_name(key).c_str());
658	        }
659	        return CMD_EXEC_FATAL_ERROR;
660	      }
661	
662	      if (false == module_manager.valid_module_instance_id(
663	                     top_module, instance_info.first, instance_info.second)) {
664	        if (!fabric_key.key_alias(key).empty()) {
665	          VTR_LOG_ERROR("Invalid key alias '%s'!\n",
666	                        fabric_key.key_alias(key).c_str());
667	        } else {
668	          VTR_LOG_ERROR("Invalid key value '%ld'!\n", instance_info.second);
669	        }
670	        return CMD_EXEC_FATAL_ERROR;
671	      }
672	
673	      /* If the the child has not configuration bits, error out */
674	      if (0 == find_module_num_config_bits(
675	                 module_manager, instance_info.first, circuit_lib,
676	                 config_protocol.memory_model(), config_protocol.type())) {
677	        if (!fabric_key.key_alias(key).empty()) {
678	          VTR_LOG_ERROR(
679	            "Invalid key alias '%s' which has zero configuration bits!\n",
680	            fabric_key.key_alias(key).c_str());
681	        } else {
682	          VTR_LOG_ERROR(
683	            "Invalid key name '%s' which has zero configuration bits!\n",
684	            fabric_key.key_name(key).c_str());
685	        }
686	        return CMD_EXEC_FATAL_ERROR;
687	      }
688	
689	      /* Now we can add the child to configurable children of the top module */
690	      module_manager.add_configurable_child(
691	        top_module, instance_info.first, instance_info.second,
692	        ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::UNIFIED,
693	        fabric_key.key_coordinate(key));
694	      module_manager.add_configurable_child_to_region(
695	        top_module, top_module_config_region, instance_info.first,
696	        instance_info.second, curr_configurable_child_id);
697	      curr_configurable_child_id++;
698	    }
699	  }
700	
701	  return CMD_EXEC_SUCCESS;
702	}
703	
704	/********************************************************************
705	 * Find the number of configuration bits in each region of
706	 * the top-level module.
707	 *
708	 * Note:
709	 *   - This function should be called after the configurable children
710	 *     is loaded to the top-level module!
711	 ********************************************************************/
712	TopModuleNumConfigBits find_top_module_regional_num_config_bit(
713	  const ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& top_module,
714	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, const CircuitModelId& sram_model,
715	  const e_config_protocol_type& config_protocol_type) {
716	  /* Initialize the number of configuration bits for each region */
717	  TopModuleNumConfigBits num_config_bits(
718	    module_manager.regions(top_module).size(), std::pair<size_t, size_t>(0, 0));
719	
720	  switch (config_protocol_type) {
721	    case CONFIG_MEM_STANDALONE:
722	    case CONFIG_MEM_SCAN_CHAIN:
723	    case CONFIG_MEM_MEMORY_BANK: {
724	      /* For flatten, chain and memory bank configuration protocol
725	       * The number of configuration bits is the sum of configuration bits
726	       * per configurable children in each region
727	       */
728	      for (const ConfigRegionId& config_region :
729	           module_manager.regions(top_module)) {
730	        for (const ModuleId& child_module :
731	             module_manager.region_configurable_children(top_module,
732	                                                         config_region)) {
733	          num_config_bits[config_region].first += find_module_num_config_bits(
734	            module_manager, child_module, circuit_lib, sram_model,
735	            config_protocol_type);
736	        }
737	      }
738	      break;
739	    }
740	    case CONFIG_MEM_QL_MEMORY_BANK: {
741	      /* For QL memory bank: we will use the row and column information for each
742	       * configuration child in order to identify the number of unique BLs and
743	       * WLs In this configuration protocol,
744	       * - all the configurable child in the same row will share the same WLs
745	       * - the number of WLs per row is limited by the configurable child which
746	       * requires most WLs
747	       * - each row has independent WLs
748	       * - all the configurable child in the same column will share the same BLs
749	       * - the number of BLs per column is limited by the configurable child
750	       * which requires most BLs
751	       * - each column has independent BLs
752	       */
753	      for (const ConfigRegionId& config_region :
754	           module_manager.regions(top_module)) {
755	        std::map<int, size_t> num_bls_per_tile =
756	          compute_memory_bank_regional_bitline_numbers_per_tile(
757	            module_manager, top_module, config_region, circuit_lib, sram_model);
758	        std::map<int, size_t> num_wls_per_tile =
759	          compute_memory_bank_regional_wordline_numbers_per_tile(
760	            module_manager, top_module, config_region, circuit_lib, sram_model);
761	        for (const auto& kv : num_bls_per_tile) {
762	          num_config_bits[config_region].first += kv.second;
763	        }
764	        for (const auto& kv : num_wls_per_tile) {
765	          num_config_bits[config_region].second += kv.second;
766	        }
767	      }
768	      break;
769	    }
770	    case CONFIG_MEM_FRAME_BASED: {
771	      /* For frame-based configuration protocol
772	       * The number of configuration bits is the sum of
773	       * - the maximum of configuration bits among configurable children
774	       * - and the number of configurable children
775	       */
776	      for (const ConfigRegionId& config_region :
777	           module_manager.regions(top_module)) {
778	        for (const ModuleId& child_module :
779	             module_manager.region_configurable_children(top_module,
780	                                                         config_region)) {
781	          size_t temp_num_config_bits = find_module_num_config_bits(
782	            module_manager, child_module, circuit_lib, sram_model,
783	            config_protocol_type);
784	          num_config_bits[config_region].first = std::max(
785	            temp_num_config_bits, num_config_bits[config_region].first);
786	        }
787	
788	        /* If there are more than 2 configurable children, we need a decoder
789	         * Otherwise, we can just short wire the address port to the children
790	         */
791	        if (1 < module_manager
792	                  .region_configurable_children(top_module, config_region)
793	                  .size()) {
794	          num_config_bits[config_region].first +=
795	            find_mux_local_decoder_addr_size(
796	              module_manager
797	                .region_configurable_children(top_module, config_region)
798	                .size());
799	        }
800	      }
801	
802	      break;
803	    }
804	
805	    default:
806	      VTR_LOG_ERROR("Invalid type of SRAM organization !\n");
807	      exit(1);
808	  }
809	
810	  return num_config_bits;
811	}
812	
813	/********************************************************************
814	 * Generate a list of ports that are used for SRAM configuration
815	 * to the top-level module
816	 * 1. Standalone SRAMs:
817	 *    use the suggested port_size
818	 * 2. Scan-chain Flip-flops:
819	 *    IMPORTANT: the port size will be limited by the number of configurable
820	 *regions
821	 * 3. Memory decoders:
822	 *    use the suggested port_size
823	 ********************************************************************/
824	static size_t generate_top_module_sram_port_size(
825	  const ConfigProtocol& config_protocol, const size_t& num_config_bits) {
826	  size_t sram_port_size = num_config_bits;
827	
828	  switch (config_protocol.type()) {
829	    case CONFIG_MEM_STANDALONE:
830	      break;
831	    case CONFIG_MEM_SCAN_CHAIN:
832	    case CONFIG_MEM_QL_MEMORY_BANK:
833	    case CONFIG_MEM_MEMORY_BANK:
834	    case CONFIG_MEM_FRAME_BASED:
835	      /* CCFF head/tail, data input could be multi-bit ports */
836	      sram_port_size = config_protocol.num_regions();
837	      break;
838	    default:
839	      VTR_LOGF_ERROR(__FILE__, __LINE__,
840	                     "Invalid type of SRAM organization!\n");
841	      exit(1);
842	  }
843	
844	  return sram_port_size;
845	}
846	
847	/********************************************************************
848	 * Add a list of ports that are used for SRAM configuration to the FPGA
849	 * top-level module
850	 * The type and names of added ports strongly depend on the
851	 * organization of SRAMs.
852	 * - Standalone SRAMs:
853	 *   two ports will be added, which are BL and WL
854	 * - Scan-chain Flip-flops:
855	 *   two ports will be added, which are the head of scan-chain
856	 *   and the tail of scan-chain
857	 *   IMPORTANT: the port size will be forced to 1 in this case
858	 *              because the head and tail are both 1-bit ports!!!
859	 * - Memory decoders:
860	 *   - An enable signal
861	 *   - A BL address port
862	 *   - A WL address port
863	 *   - A data-in port for the BL decoder
864	 * - QL memory decoder:
865	 *   - An enable signal
866	 *   - An BL address port
867	 *   - A WL address port
868	 *   - A data-in port for the BL decoder
869	 *   @note In this memory decoders, the address size will be computed in a
870	 *different way than the regular one
871	 * - Frame-based memory:
872	 *   - An Enable signal
873	 *   - An address port, whose size depends on the number of config bits
874	 *     and the maximum size of address ports of configurable children
875	 *   - An data_in port (single-bit)
876	 ********************************************************************/
877	void add_top_module_sram_ports(
878	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& module_id,
879	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, const CircuitModelId& sram_model,
880	  const ConfigProtocol& config_protocol,
881	  const MemoryBankShiftRegisterBanks& blwl_sr_banks,
882	  const TopModuleNumConfigBits& num_config_bits) {
883	  std::vector<std::string> sram_port_names =
884	    generate_sram_port_names(circuit_lib, sram_model, config_protocol.type());
885	  size_t total_num_config_bits = 0;
886	  for (const auto& curr_num_config_bits : num_config_bits) {
887	    total_num_config_bits += curr_num_config_bits.first;
888	  }
889	  size_t sram_port_size =
890	    generate_top_module_sram_port_size(config_protocol, total_num_config_bits);
891	
892	  /* Add ports to the module manager */
893	  switch (config_protocol.type()) {
894	    case CONFIG_MEM_STANDALONE: {
895	      for (const std::string& sram_port_name : sram_port_names) {
896	        /* Add generated ports to the ModuleManager */
897	        BasicPort sram_port(sram_port_name, sram_port_size);
898	        module_manager.add_port(module_id, sram_port,
899	                                ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
900	      }
901	      break;
902	    }
903	    case CONFIG_MEM_MEMORY_BANK: {
904	      BasicPort en_port(std::string(DECODER_ENABLE_PORT_NAME), 1);
905	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, en_port,
906	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
907	
908	      /* BL address size is the largest among all the regions */
909	      size_t bl_addr_size = 0;
910	      for (const ConfigRegionId& config_region :
911	           module_manager.regions(module_id)) {
912	        bl_addr_size = std::max(
913	          bl_addr_size,
914	          find_memory_decoder_addr_size(num_config_bits[config_region].first));
915	      }
916	      BasicPort bl_addr_port(std::string(DECODER_BL_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME),
917	                             bl_addr_size);
918	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, bl_addr_port,
919	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
920	
921	      /* WL address size is the largest among all the regions */
922	      size_t wl_addr_size = 0;
923	      for (const ConfigRegionId& config_region :
924	           module_manager.regions(module_id)) {
925	        wl_addr_size = std::max(
926	          wl_addr_size,
927	          find_memory_decoder_addr_size(num_config_bits[config_region].first));
928	      }
929	      BasicPort wl_addr_port(std::string(DECODER_WL_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME),
930	                             wl_addr_size);
931	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, wl_addr_port,
932	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
933	
934	      /* Data input should be dependent on the number of configuration regions*/
935	      BasicPort din_port(std::string(DECODER_DATA_IN_PORT_NAME),
936	                         config_protocol.num_regions());
937	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, din_port,
938	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
939	
940	      break;
941	    }
942	    case CONFIG_MEM_QL_MEMORY_BANK: {
943	      add_top_module_ql_memory_bank_sram_ports(module_manager, module_id,
944	                                               circuit_lib, config_protocol,
945	                                               blwl_sr_banks, num_config_bits);
946	      break;
947	    }
948	    case CONFIG_MEM_SCAN_CHAIN: {
949	      /* Note that configuration chain tail is an output while head is an input
950	       * IMPORTANT: this is co-designed with function generate_sram_port_names()
951	       * If the return vector is changed, the following codes MUST be adapted!
952	       */
953	      VTR_ASSERT(2 == sram_port_names.size());
954	      size_t port_counter = 0;
955	      for (const std::string& sram_port_name : sram_port_names) {
956	        /* Add generated ports to the ModuleManager */
957	        BasicPort sram_port(sram_port_name, sram_port_size);
958	        if (0 == port_counter) {
959	          module_manager.add_port(module_id, sram_port,
960	                                  ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
961	        } else {
962	          VTR_ASSERT(1 == port_counter);
963	          module_manager.add_port(module_id, sram_port,
964	                                  ModuleManager::MODULE_OUTPUT_PORT);
965	        }
966	        port_counter++;
967	      }
968	      break;
969	    }
970	    case CONFIG_MEM_FRAME_BASED: {
971	      BasicPort en_port(std::string(DECODER_ENABLE_PORT_NAME), 1);
972	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, en_port,
973	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
974	
975	      size_t max_num_config_bits = 0;
976	      for (const auto& curr_num_config_bits : num_config_bits) {
977	        max_num_config_bits =
978	          std::max(max_num_config_bits, curr_num_config_bits.first);
979	      }
980	
981	      BasicPort addr_port(std::string(DECODER_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME),
982	                          max_num_config_bits);
983	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, addr_port,
984	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
985	
986	      BasicPort din_port(std::string(DECODER_DATA_IN_PORT_NAME),
987	                         sram_port_size);
988	      module_manager.add_port(module_id, din_port,
989	                              ModuleManager::MODULE_INPUT_PORT);
990	
991	      break;
992	    }
993	    default:
994	      VTR_LOGF_ERROR(__FILE__, __LINE__,
995	                     "Invalid type of SRAM organization !\n");
996	      exit(1);
997	  }
998	}
999	
1000	/*********************************************************************
1001	 * Top-level function to add nets for memory banks
1002	 * Each configuration region has independent memory bank circuitry
1003	 * - Find the number of BLs and WLs required for each region
1004	 * - Create BL and WL decoders, and add them to decoder library
1005	 * - Create nets to connect from top-level module inputs to inputs of decoders
1006	 * - Create nets to connect from outputs of decoders to BL/WL of configurable
1007	 *children
1008	 *
1009	 * Detailed schematic of how memory banks are connected in the top-level:
1010	 * Consider a random Region X, local BL address lines are aligned to the LSB of
1011	 *the top-level BL address lines
1012	 *
1013	 *            top_bl_addr[N-1:0]
1014	 *                 ^
1015	 *                 | local_bl_addr[N-1:0]
1016	 *                 |
1017	 *           +-----+------------------+
1018	 *           |     |                  |
1019	 *           |  +-------------------+ |
1020	 *           |  | Word Line Decoder | |
1021	 *           |  +-------------------+ |
1022	 *           |                        |
1023	 *
1024	 * The BL/WL decoders should have the same circuit designs no matter what region
1025	 * they are placed even when the number of configuration bits are different
1026	 * from one region to another!
1027	 * This is designed to avoid any address collision between memory banks
1028	 * since they are programmed in the same clock cycle
1029	 * For example:
1030	 *   - Memory Bank A has 36 memory cells.
1031	 *     Its BL decoder has 3 address bit and 6 data output bit
1032	 *     Its WL decoder has 3 address bit and 6 data output bit
1033	 *   - Memory Bank B has 16 memory cells.
1034	 *     Its BL decoder has 2 address bit and 4 data output bit
1035	 *     Its WL decoder has 2 address bit and 4 data output bit
1036	 *   - If we try to program the 36th memory cell in bank A
1037	 *     the BL address will be 3'b110
1038	 *     the WL address will be 3'b110
1039	 *     the data input will be 1'b0
1040	 *   - If we try to program the 4th memory cell in bank A
1041	 *     the BL address will be 3'b010
1042	 *     the WL address will be 3'b010
1043	 *     the data input will be 1'b1
1044	 *     However, in both cases, this will trigger a parasitic programming in bank
1045	 *B the BL address will be 2'b10 the WL address will be 2'b10 Assume the data
1046	 *input is expected to be 1'b1 for bank B but it will be overwritten to 1'b0
1047	 *when programming the 36th cell in bank A!
1048	 *
1049	 * Detailed schematic of each memory bank:
1050	 *
1051	 *                            WL_enable  WL address
1052	 *                               |           |
1053	 *                               v           v
1054	 *                           +-----------------------------------------------+
1055	 *                           |     Word Line Decoder                         |
1056	 *                           +-----------------------------------------------+
1057	 *               +---------+    |             |                   |
1058	 *  BL           |         |    |             |                   |
1059	 *  enable  ---->|         |-----------+--------------+----  ...  |------+-->
1060	 *BL[0] |         |    |      |      |       |           |      | |         | |
1061	 *v      |       v           |      v | Bit     |    |   +------+  |   +------+
1062	 *|  +------+ BL           | Line    |    +-->| SRAM |  +-->| SRAM |        +->|
1063	 *SRAM | address ---->| Decoder |    |   | [0]  |  |   |  [1] |   ...  |  |  [i]
1064	 *| |         |    |   +------+  |   +------+        |  +------+ |         | |
1065	 *|                   | |         |-----------+--------------+----  ---  |
1066	 *-----+--> BL[1] |         |    |      |      |       |           |      | | |
1067	 *|      v      |       v           |      v |         |    |   +------+  |
1068	 *+------+        |  +------+ |         |    +-->| SRAM |  |   | SRAM | +->|
1069	 *SRAM | |         |    |   | [x]  |  |   | [x+1]|   ...  |  | [x+i]| | |    |
1070	 *+------+  |   +------+        |  +------+ |         |    | | |         |    |
1071	 *...    ...    ...           |    ... |         |    |             | | |
1072	 *|-----------+--------------+----  ---  | -----+--> BL[y] |         |    | | |
1073	 *|           |      | |         |    |      v      |       v           |      v
1074	 *               |         |    |   +------+  |   +------+        |  +------+
1075	 *               |         |    +-->| SRAM |  +-->| SRAM |        +->| SRAM |
1076	 *               |         |    |   | [y]  |  |   |[y+1] |   ...  |  |[y+i] |
1077	 *               |         |    |   +------+  |   +------+        |  +------+
1078	 *  BL           |         |    v             v                   v
1079	 *  data_in ---->|         |  WL[0]          WL[1]              WL[i]
1080	 *               +---------+
1081	 *
1082	 **********************************************************************/
1083	static void add_top_module_nets_cmos_memory_bank_config_bus(
1084	  ModuleManager& module_manager, DecoderLibrary& decoder_lib,
1085	  const ModuleId& top_module, const TopModuleNumConfigBits& num_config_bits) {
1086	  /* Find Enable port from the top-level module */
1087	  ModulePortId en_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1088	    top_module, std::string(DECODER_ENABLE_PORT_NAME));
1089	  BasicPort en_port_info = module_manager.module_port(top_module, en_port);
1090	
1091	  /* Find data-in port from the top-level module */
1092	  ModulePortId din_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1093	    top_module, std::string(DECODER_DATA_IN_PORT_NAME));
1094	  BasicPort din_port_info = module_manager.module_port(top_module, din_port);
1095	
1096	  /* Data in port should match the number of configuration regions */
1097	  VTR_ASSERT(din_port_info.get_width() ==
1098	             module_manager.regions(top_module).size());
1099	
1100	  /* Find BL and WL address port from the top-level module */
1101	  ModulePortId bl_addr_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1102	    top_module, std::string(DECODER_BL_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME));
1103	  BasicPort bl_addr_port_info =
1104	    module_manager.module_port(top_module, bl_addr_port);
1105	
1106	  ModulePortId wl_addr_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1107	    top_module, std::string(DECODER_WL_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME));
1108	  BasicPort wl_addr_port_info =
1109	    module_manager.module_port(top_module, wl_addr_port);
1110	
1111	  /* Find the top-level number of BLs and WLs required to access each memory bit
1112	   */
1113	  size_t bl_addr_size = bl_addr_port_info.get_width();
1114	  size_t wl_addr_size = wl_addr_port_info.get_width();
1115	
1116	  /* Each memory bank has a unified number of BL/WLs */
1117	  size_t num_bls = 0;
1118	  for (const auto& curr_config_bits : num_config_bits) {
1119	    num_bls = std::max(
1120	      num_bls, find_memory_decoder_data_size(curr_config_bits.first, 0, true));
1121	  }
1122	
1123	  size_t num_wls = 0;
1124	  for (const auto& curr_config_bits : num_config_bits) {
1125	    num_wls = std::max(
1126	      num_wls, find_memory_decoder_data_size(curr_config_bits.first, 0, false));
1127	  }
1128	
1129	  /* Create separated memory bank circuitry, i.e., BL/WL decoders for each
1130	   * region */
1131	  for (const ConfigRegionId& config_region :
1132	       module_manager.regions(top_module)) {
1133	    /**************************************************************
1134	     * Add the BL decoder module
1135	     * Search the decoder library
1136	     * If we find one, we use the module.
1137	     * Otherwise, we create one and add it to the decoder library
1138	     */
1139	    DecoderId bl_decoder_id =
1140	      decoder_lib.find_decoder(bl_addr_size, num_bls, true, true, false, false);
1141	    if (DecoderId::INVALID() == bl_decoder_id) {
1142	      bl_decoder_id = decoder_lib.add_decoder(bl_addr_size, num_bls, true, true,
1143	                                              false, false);
1144	    }
1145	    VTR_ASSERT(DecoderId::INVALID() != bl_decoder_id);
1146	
1147	    /* Create a module if not existed yet */
1148	    std::string bl_decoder_module_name =
1149	      generate_memory_decoder_with_data_in_subckt_name(bl_addr_size, num_bls);
1150	    ModuleId bl_decoder_module =
1151	      module_manager.find_module(bl_decoder_module_name);
1152	    if (ModuleId::INVALID() == bl_decoder_module) {
1153	      /* BL decoder has the same ports as the frame-based decoders
1154	       * We reuse it here
1155	       */
1156	      bl_decoder_module = build_bl_memory_decoder_module(
1157	        module_manager, decoder_lib, bl_decoder_id);
1158	    }
1159	    VTR_ASSERT(ModuleId::INVALID() != bl_decoder_module);
1160	    size_t curr_bl_decoder_instance_id =
1161	      module_manager.num_instance(top_module, bl_decoder_module);
1162	    module_manager.add_child_module(top_module, bl_decoder_module, false);
1163	
1164	    /**************************************************************
1165	     * Add the WL decoder module
1166	     * Search the decoder library
1167	     * If we find one, we use the module.
1168	     * Otherwise, we create one and add it to the decoder library
1169	     */
1170	    DecoderId wl_decoder_id = decoder_lib.find_decoder(
1171	      wl_addr_size, num_wls, true, false, false, false);
1172	    if (DecoderId::INVALID() == wl_decoder_id) {
1173	      wl_decoder_id = decoder_lib.add_decoder(wl_addr_size, num_wls, true,
1174	                                              false, false, false);
1175	    }
1176	    VTR_ASSERT(DecoderId::INVALID() != wl_decoder_id);
1177	
1178	    /* Create a module if not existed yet */
1179	    std::string wl_decoder_module_name =
1180	      generate_memory_decoder_subckt_name(wl_addr_size, num_wls);
1181	    ModuleId wl_decoder_module =
1182	      module_manager.find_module(wl_decoder_module_name);
1183	    if (ModuleId::INVALID() == wl_decoder_module) {
1184	      /* BL decoder has the same ports as the frame-based decoders
1185	       * We reuse it here
1186	       */
1187	      wl_decoder_module = build_wl_memory_decoder_module(
1188	        module_manager, decoder_lib, wl_decoder_id);
1189	    }
1190	    VTR_ASSERT(ModuleId::INVALID() != wl_decoder_module);
1191	    size_t curr_wl_decoder_instance_id =
1192	      module_manager.num_instance(top_module, wl_decoder_module);
1193	    module_manager.add_child_module(top_module, wl_decoder_module, false);
1194	
1195	    /**************************************************************
1196	     * Add module nets from the top module to BL decoder's inputs
1197	     */
1198	    ModulePortId bl_decoder_en_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1199	      bl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_ENABLE_PORT_NAME));
1200	    BasicPort bl_decoder_en_port_info =
1201	      module_manager.module_port(bl_decoder_module, bl_decoder_en_port);
1202	
1203	    ModulePortId bl_decoder_addr_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1204	      bl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME));
1205	    BasicPort bl_decoder_addr_port_info =
1206	      module_manager.module_port(bl_decoder_module, bl_decoder_addr_port);
1207	
1208	    ModulePortId bl_decoder_din_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1209	      bl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_DATA_IN_PORT_NAME));
1210	    BasicPort bl_decoder_din_port_info =
1211	      module_manager.module_port(bl_decoder_module, bl_decoder_din_port);
1212	
1213	    /* Data in port of the local BL decoder should always be 1 */
1214	    VTR_ASSERT(1 == bl_decoder_din_port_info.get_width());
1215	
1216	    /* Top module Enable port -> BL Decoder Enable port */
1217	    add_module_bus_nets(module_manager, top_module, top_module, 0, en_port,
1218	                        bl_decoder_module, curr_bl_decoder_instance_id,
1219	                        bl_decoder_en_port);
1220	
1221	    /* Top module Address port -> BL Decoder Address port */
1222	    add_module_bus_nets(module_manager, top_module, top_module, 0, bl_addr_port,
1223	                        bl_decoder_module, curr_bl_decoder_instance_id,
1224	                        bl_decoder_addr_port);
1225	
1226	    /* Top module data_in port -> BL Decoder data_in port:
1227	     * Note that each region has independent data_in connection from the
1228	     * top-level module The pin index is the configuration region index
1229	     */
1230	    ModuleNetId din_net = create_module_source_pin_net(
1231	      module_manager, top_module, top_module, 0, din_port,
1232	      din_port_info.pins()[size_t(config_region)]);
1233	    VTR_ASSERT(ModuleNetId::INVALID() != din_net);
1234	
1235	    /* Configure the net sink */
1236	    module_manager.add_module_net_sink(
1237	      top_module, din_net, bl_decoder_module, curr_bl_decoder_instance_id,
1238	      bl_decoder_din_port, bl_decoder_din_port_info.pins()[0]);
1239	
1240	    /**************************************************************
1241	     * Add module nets from the top module to WL decoder's inputs
1242	     */
1243	    ModulePortId wl_decoder_en_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1244	      wl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_ENABLE_PORT_NAME));
1245	    BasicPort wl_decoder_en_port_info =
1246	      module_manager.module_port(wl_decoder_module, wl_decoder_en_port);
1247	
1248	    ModulePortId wl_decoder_addr_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1249	      wl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_ADDRESS_PORT_NAME));
1250	    BasicPort wl_decoder_addr_port_info =
1251	      module_manager.module_port(wl_decoder_module, bl_decoder_addr_port);
1252	
1253	    /* Top module Enable port -> WL Decoder Enable port */
1254	    add_module_bus_nets(module_manager, top_module, top_module, 0, en_port,
1255	                        wl_decoder_module, curr_wl_decoder_instance_id,
1256	                        wl_decoder_en_port);
1257	
1258	    /* Top module Address port -> WL Decoder Address port */
1259	    add_module_bus_nets(module_manager, top_module, top_module, 0, wl_addr_port,
1260	                        wl_decoder_module, curr_wl_decoder_instance_id,
1261	                        wl_decoder_addr_port);
1262	
1263	    /**************************************************************
1264	     * Add nets from BL data out to each configurable child
1265	     */
1266	    size_t cur_bl_index = 0;
1267	
1268	    ModulePortId bl_decoder_dout_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1269	      bl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_DATA_OUT_PORT_NAME));
1270	    BasicPort bl_decoder_dout_port_info =
1271	      module_manager.module_port(bl_decoder_module, bl_decoder_dout_port);
1272	
1273	    for (size_t child_id = 0;
1274	         child_id <
1275	         module_manager.region_configurable_children(top_module, config_region)
1276	           .size();
1277	         ++child_id) {
1278	      ModuleId child_module = module_manager.region_configurable_children(
1279	        top_module, config_region)[child_id];
1280	      size_t child_instance =
1281	        module_manager.region_configurable_child_instances(
1282	          top_module, config_region)[child_id];
1283	
1284	      /* Find the BL port */
1285	      ModulePortId child_bl_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1286	        child_module, std::string(MEMORY_BL_PORT_NAME));
1287	      BasicPort child_bl_port_info =
1288	        module_manager.module_port(child_module, child_bl_port);
1289	
1290	      for (const size_t& sink_bl_pin : child_bl_port_info.pins()) {
1291	        /* Find the BL decoder data index:
1292	         * It should be the residual when divided by the number of BLs
1293	         */
1294	        size_t bl_pin_id = std::floor(cur_bl_index / num_bls);
1295	        if (!(bl_pin_id < bl_decoder_dout_port_info.pins().size()))
1296	          VTR_ASSERT(bl_pin_id < bl_decoder_dout_port_info.pins().size());
1297	
1298	        /* Create net */
1299	        ModuleNetId net = create_module_source_pin_net(
1300	          module_manager, top_module, bl_decoder_module,
1301	          curr_bl_decoder_instance_id, bl_decoder_dout_port,
1302	          bl_decoder_dout_port_info.pins()[bl_pin_id]);
1303	        VTR_ASSERT(ModuleNetId::INVALID() != net);
1304	
1305	        /* Add net sink */
1306	        module_manager.add_module_net_sink(top_module, net, child_module,
1307	                                           child_instance, child_bl_port,
1308	                                           sink_bl_pin);
1309	
1310	        /* Increment the BL index */
1311	        cur_bl_index++;
1312	      }
1313	    }
1314	
1315	    /**************************************************************
1316	     * Add nets from WL data out to each configurable child
1317	     */
1318	    size_t cur_wl_index = 0;
1319	
1320	    ModulePortId wl_decoder_dout_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1321	      wl_decoder_module, std::string(DECODER_DATA_OUT_PORT_NAME));
1322	    BasicPort wl_decoder_dout_port_info =
1323	      module_manager.module_port(wl_decoder_module, wl_decoder_dout_port);
1324	
1325	    for (size_t child_id = 0;
1326	         child_id <
1327	         module_manager.region_configurable_children(top_module, config_region)
1328	           .size();
1329	         ++child_id) {
1330	      ModuleId child_module = module_manager.region_configurable_children(
1331	        top_module, config_region)[child_id];
1332	      size_t child_instance =
1333	        module_manager.region_configurable_child_instances(
1334	          top_module, config_region)[child_id];
1335	
1336	      /* Find the WL port */
1337	      ModulePortId child_wl_port = module_manager.find_module_port(
1338	        child_module, std::string(MEMORY_WL_PORT_NAME));
1339	      BasicPort child_wl_port_info =
1340	        module_manager.module_port(child_module, child_wl_port);
1341	
1342	      for (const size_t& sink_wl_pin : child_wl_port_info.pins()) {
1343	        /* Find the BL decoder data index:
1344	         * It should be the residual when divided by the number of BLs
1345	         */
1346	        size_t wl_pin_id = cur_wl_index % num_wls;
1347	
1348	        /* Create net */
1349	        ModuleNetId net = create_module_source_pin_net(
1350	          module_manager, top_module, wl_decoder_module,
1351	          curr_wl_decoder_instance_id, wl_decoder_dout_port,
1352	          wl_decoder_dout_port_info.pins()[wl_pin_id]);
1353	        VTR_ASSERT(ModuleNetId::INVALID() != net);
1354	
1355	        /* Add net sink */
1356	        module_manager.add_module_net_sink(top_module, net, child_module,
1357	                                           child_instance, child_wl_port,
1358	                                           sink_wl_pin);
1359	
1360	        /* Increment the WL index */
1361	        cur_wl_index++;
1362	      }
1363	    }
1364	
1365	    /**************************************************************
1366	     * Add the BL and WL decoders to the end of configurable children list
1367	     * Note: this MUST be done after adding all the module nets to other regular
1368	     * configurable children
1369	     */
1370	    module_manager.add_configurable_child(
1371	      top_module, bl_decoder_module, curr_bl_decoder_instance_id,
1372	      ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL);
1373	    module_manager.add_configurable_child_to_region(
1374	      top_module, config_region, bl_decoder_module, curr_bl_decoder_instance_id,
1375	      module_manager
1376	          .configurable_children(top_module,
1377	                                 ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
1378	          .size() -
1379	        1);
1380	
1381	    module_manager.add_configurable_child(
1382	      top_module, wl_decoder_module, curr_wl_decoder_instance_id,
1383	      ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL);
1384	    module_manager.add_configurable_child_to_region(
1385	      top_module, config_region, wl_decoder_module, curr_wl_decoder_instance_id,
1386	      module_manager
1387	          .configurable_children(top_module,
1388	                                 ModuleManager::e_config_child_type::PHYSICAL)
1389	          .size() -
1390	        1);
1391	  }
1392	}
